_comment1: AI Guardian Configuration - MCP Server and Skill Permissions
_comment6: - Built-in tools (Read, Write, Bash, etc.): ALLOW by default
_comment7: - Skills (Skill): BLOCK by default (must be explicitly allowed)
_comment8: - MCP Servers (mcp__*): BLOCK by default (must be explicitly allowed)
_comment15: No configuration can disable these protections - they are hardcoded

permissions._comment: Tool permission enforcement - WHERE THE RULES LIVE
permissions._comment2: Controls which TOOLS can run (Skills, MCP servers, Bash, Write, etc.)
permissions._comment3: This is the actual enforcement layer - rules here are checked when tools execute
permissions._comment4: Works with permissions_directories (auto-discovery feeds INTO this section)
permissions._comment5: NEW unified structure in v1.4.0: combines enabled flag and rules in one object

auto_directory_rules._comment: Auto-generate directory rules from skill permissions (Issue #144)
auto_directory_rules._comment2: allow_symlinks: In container environments (e.g., carbonite), skills are installed as symlinks.
auto_directory_rules._comment3: Set to false to skip all symlinks (original behavior). Broken symlinks are always skipped.

rules._comment: MCP tools - must be explicitly allowed

permissions_directories._comment: OPTIONAL/ADVANCED: Auto-discover tool permissions - HOW TO AUTO-POPULATE RULES
permissions_directories._comment2: Scans directories/GitHub repos for permission files → merges discovered rules INTO permissions.rules
permissions_directories._comment3: This is NOT about blocking directories - that's directory_rules (see below)
permissions_directories._comment4: Data flow: scan directories → discover permission files → generate rules → merge into permissions.rules
permissions_directories._comment5: Most users should use remote_configs instead (easier to manage)
permissions_directories._comment6: Use this for local development or when you can't pre-list all items

remote_configs._comment: RECOMMENDED: Load policies from remote URLs (enterprise/team policies)
remote_configs._comment2: This is the preferred way to manage permissions - easier than directory discovery
remote_configs._comment3: Remote policy can include complete permissions, no need for dynamic discovery
remote_configs._comment6: Remote URLs are loaded from the FIRST source found (highest to lowest priority):
remote_configs._comment13: If a higher priority source exists, THIS file's remote URLs are IGNORED.
remote_configs._comment14: This prevents users from bypassing enterprise policies by adding their own URLs.
remote_configs._comment22: # Users can no longer add their own remote URLs (enforced by cascading priority)
remote_configs._comment24: See docs/CONFIGURATION.md for complete cascading priority documentation

urls._comment: Enterprise-wide policy with complete permissions list

secret_scanning._comment: NEW in v1.4.0: Secret scanning with Gitleaks control
secret_scanning._comment2: Controls whether secret scanning is performed
secret_scanning._comment3: Supports both boolean (permanent) and time-based (temporary) formats
secret_scanning._pattern_server_comment: NEW in v1.7.0: pattern_server nested under secret_scanning (was at root level)

pattern_server._comment: Optional: Enhanced secret detection patterns from a pattern server
pattern_server._comment2: This is an ADVANCED feature - most users should use default Gitleaks patterns
pattern_server._comment3: Presence of this section = enabled. To disable: set to null or remove entirely.
pattern_server._comment4: Cascade/fallback: pattern server → project .gitleaks.toml → Gitleaks defaults
pattern_server._usage: Remove this entire 'pattern_server' section if you don't need it (will use defaults)

_immutable_example_remote_config._comment: In remote config: Enforce custom pattern server that cannot be overridden
_immutable_example_remote_config._explanation: Enterprise pattern server - local configs cannot disable or override

pattern_server._url_comment: Set to your pattern server URL to enable

_leaktk_example_config._comment: RECOMMENDED: Use LeakTK community patterns (free, no auth required)

pattern_server._warn_on_failure_comment: Show warning when pattern server fails (auth, network, etc). Default: true. Set to false to suppress warnings.

auth._comment: Token auth: Set env var OR save to token_file (pick one method)
auth._comment2: Get token from your pattern server's web interface

cache._comment: Patterns auto-refresh every 12h, expire after 7 days

prompt_injection._comment2: Protects against prompt injection attacks that try to manipulate AI behavior
prompt_injection._comment3: Default: Enabled with heuristic detection (local, fast, privacy-preserving)
prompt_injection._comment4: NEW in v1.4.0: Supports time-based disabling for debugging/testing

_extended_format_example.reason: Testing documentation with prompt injection examples

_immutable_example_remote_config._comment: In remote config: Mark entire section as immutable to enforce enterprise policy
_immutable_example_remote_config._explanation: Local configs cannot change prompt injection settings when immutable is true

prompt_injection._detector_note: heuristic = local patterns (default, <1ms), rebuff/llm-guard = ML-based (requires setup)
prompt_injection._sensitivity_note: low = very obvious attacks only, medium = balanced, high = more aggressive
prompt_injection._allowlist_note: Add regex patterns here to ignore false positives, e.g. ["test:.*", "system:test.*"]
prompt_injection._custom_patterns_note: Add additional detection patterns here, e.g. ["company_secret_.*"]

_detection_patterns._comment: Built-in patterns detect common attack categories:
_detection_patterns._security_note: Specific examples are not provided to prevent misuse. See README FAQ for guidance on researching prompt injection patterns safely.

scan_pii._comment: PII detection for GDPR/CCPA compliance (NEW in v1.6.0)
scan_pii._comment2: Scans user prompts, file reads, and tool outputs for personally identifiable information
scan_pii._comment3: Enabled by default. Use ignore_files to skip test files with example PII data.
scan_pii._comment4: action: 'block' = block in UserPromptSubmit/PreToolUse, warn in PostToolUse (default)
scan_pii._comment5: action: 'log-only' = warn but allow in all hooks

ssrf_protection._comment: ⚠️ IMPORTANT: Pattern-based filtering only - cannot replace network-level security
ssrf_protection._limitation_1: Can only inspect command strings and tool parameters
ssrf_protection._limitation_2: Cannot detect MCP server internal network calls
ssrf_protection._limitation_3: Cannot block HTTP redirects or dynamic URL construction
ssrf_protection._recommendation: For comprehensive SSRF protection, use firewall rules and MCP sandboxing
ssrf_protection._learn_more: See docs/SSRF_PROTECTION.md for detailed limitations and recommendations
ssrf_protection._action_note: block = prevent execution, warn = show warning but allow, log-only = silent logging
ssrf_protection._additional_blocked_ips_comment: Block additional private IP ranges beyond RFC 1918
ssrf_protection._additional_blocked_ips_note: Only works if explicitly in command/parameter string
ssrf_protection._additional_blocked_domains_comment: Block internal domains - supports exact domains, subdomain matching, and wildcard patterns (NEW in v1.5.0)
ssrf_protection._additional_blocked_domains_note: Cannot detect if MCP server internally resolves these domains
ssrf_protection._additional_blocked_domains_wildcard_sup: NEW in v1.5.0 (Issue #253): Wildcard patterns using * and ? wildcards

_additional_blocked_domains_wildcard_exa._wildcard_prefix: admin.* - Blocks admin.* with any suffix (admin.example.com, admin.local)
_additional_blocked_domains_wildcard_exa._single_char: test?.example.com - Blocks test1, test2, testa, etc. (? matches one character)

ssrf_protection._allowed_domains_comment: NEW in v1.5.0 (Issue #252): Allow-list to override additional_blocked_domains
ssrf_protection._allowed_domains_note: Evaluated AFTER deny-list. Cannot override immutable protections (metadata endpoints, dangerous schemes, private IPs)

_allowed_domains_examples._use_case_1: Allow specific internal APIs while blocking other internal domains
_allowed_domains_examples._use_case_2: Allow dev/staging servers without allowing all localhost
_allowed_domains_examples._use_case_3: Allow specific partner domains on restricted networks

ssrf_protection._allowed_domains_security_warning: ⚠️ CRITICAL: Cannot override immutable core protections
_allowed_domains_immutable_list: Dangerous URL schemes (file://, gopher://, ftp://, data://)
ssrf_protection._path_based_rules_comment: NEW in v1.6.0 (Issue #254): Path-based filtering for granular access control
ssrf_protection._path_based_rules_note: Allows blocking/allowing specific URL paths on domains

_path_based_rules_use_cases._use_case_1: Allow public API endpoints while blocking admin pages on same domain
_path_based_rules_use_cases._use_case_2: Allow health checks on internal services while blocking everything else
_path_based_rules_use_cases._use_case_3: Block old/deprecated API versions while allowing new ones

_path_based_rules_evaluation._step_2: If domain blocked AND path in allowed_paths → ALLOW
_path_based_rules_evaluation._step_3: If domain allowed AND path in blocked_paths → BLOCK
_path_based_rules_evaluation._step_4: Falls back to domain-level decision if no path rules match

_path_based_rules_glob_patterns._single_star: * matches any chars except / (e.g., /api/* matches /api/users but not /api/v1/users)
_path_based_rules_glob_patterns._double_star: ** matches any chars including / (e.g., /admin/** matches /admin/users and /admin/v1/users)
_path_based_rules_glob_patterns._exact: No wildcards = exact match (e.g., /health matches only /health)
_path_based_rules_glob_patterns._query_params: Query parameters included in match (e.g., /debug* matches /debug?verbose=true)

_path_based_rules_examples._example_1: Allow public endpoints on blocked domain

_path_based_rules_examples._example_2: Block admin paths on allowed domain

_path_based_rules_examples._example_3: Health checks on blocked internal services
_path_based_rules_examples._note: Blocks all of services.internal.corp except health/metrics/status endpoints
Allows api.example.com but blocks old API versions

_path_based_rules_security_notes: Path rules CANNOT override immutable protections (metadata endpoints, private IPs, dangerous schemes)
Trailing slashes are normalized (/admin matches /admin/ and vice versa)
Query parameters included in path matching
Use specific patterns over broad wildcards for better security
ssrf_protection._allow_localhost_comment: Set to true for local development (NEVER in production)

directory_rules._comment: NEW in v1.6.0: Order-based directory access control
directory_rules._comment2: Filesystem path access control - Controls which PATHS can be accessed/read
directory_rules._comment4: - permissions_directories: Auto-discovers TOOL permission rules (config sources)
directory_rules._comment5: - directory_rules: Blocks/allows AI access to specific PATHS (e.g., ~/.ssh, sensitive data)
directory_rules._comment6: Rules are evaluated sequentially - LAST matching rule wins
directory_rules._comment7: Default behavior: all paths allowed unless explicitly denied

_rules_examples._use_case: Block all home directory, then allow specific workspace
Allow workspace after denying all (last match wins)

_path_patterns.absolute: Use absolute paths for system directories
_path_patterns.relative: Relative paths resolved from current working directory

_evaluation_order.1: Rules evaluated sequentially from first to last
_evaluation_order.2: LAST matching rule determines access (allow or deny)
_evaluation_order.3: If no rules match → ALLOW (default permissive)
_evaluation_order.4: Pattern: deny broad → allow specific (see examples)

_security_notes: Symlinks are NOT followed for security (prevents bypass)
Paths are normalized to prevent traversal attacks
Recommended: Start with deny-all, then allow specific paths
Test rules carefully - incorrect order can expose sensitive data

directory_exclusions._comment: DEPRECATED: Use directory_rules instead (v1.6.0+). Automatically converted internally.
directory_exclusions._comment2: Filesystem path access control - Controls which PATHS can be accessed/read
directory_exclusions._comment3: ⚠️ NOT RELATED to permissions_directories (despite similar names):
directory_exclusions._comment5: - directory_exclusions/directory_rules: Blocks AI access to specific PATHS (e.g., ~/.ssh)
directory_exclusions._comment6: Directory exclusions for .ai-read-deny blocking (NEW in v1.5.0)
directory_exclusions._comment7: Disable .ai-read-deny blocking for specific directory paths
directory_exclusions._comment8: CRITICAL: .ai-read-deny markers ALWAYS take precedence over exclusions
directory_exclusions._comment9: Config-based (safe from AI manipulation, unlike marker files)
_path_notes: Paths are resolved to absolute paths
Symlinks are NOT followed for security
.ai-read-deny ALWAYS takes precedence (hardcoded, no config option to override)

_precedence_rules.1: .ai-read-deny marker ALWAYS blocks (highest priority, no exceptions)
_precedence_rules.2: If no .ai-read-deny and path matches exclusion → ALLOW (skip blocking)
_precedence_rules._critical_note: There is NO configuration option to override .ai-read-deny with exclusions
_precedence_rules._to_remove_protection: User must manually delete the .ai-read-deny file
_precedence_rules._marker_protection: AI agents cannot remove/modify .ai-read-deny files (immutable protection)

development_workspace._explanation: Allow AI access to workspace, but .ai-read-deny in subdirs still works

public_repos._explanation: Allow AI access to all public repos recursively

enterprise_policy._explanation: Corporate remote config allows approved projects

_security_warning._comment: ⚠️ IMPORTANT: Directory exclusions reduce security protection
_recommendations: Use exclusions sparingly and only for known-safe directories
AI agents cannot remove/modify .ai-read-deny files (immutable protection)
To remove protection, user must manually delete .ai-read-deny file
Exclusions should be in protected config files (not set by AI)

_environment_variables.AI_GUARDIAN_PATTERN_TOKEN: Bearer token for pattern server authentication

_pattern_matching._comment: How patterns are matched for each matcher type:

_config_precedence.2: Project local config (./.ai-guardian.json in project root)

rules._effect: Locks only Skill matcher, users can add MCP/Bash/Write rules
